description: A junior developer attempted to set up a deployment credential for a GitHub Actions CI/CD pipeline. Unaware of the Kubernetes 1.24+ changes to ServiceAccount tokens, they failed to generate a static token. To force it to work, they dangerously granted the ServiceAccount cluster-wide administrator privileges. Secure the 'github-actions' ServiceAccount in the 'webapp' namespace by revoking its cluster-admin privileges (deleting the 'github-actions-admin' ClusterRoleBinding) and implementing least-privilege access. The ServiceAccount must be restricted to managing 'deployments' and 'services' within the 'webapp' namespace using a Role named 'cicd-deployer' and a RoleBinding named 'cicd-deployer-binding'. Additionally, replace the existing invalid secret with a new Secret named 'github-actions-token' of type 'kubernetes.io/service-account-token' annotated for the 'github-actions' service account to provide a valid long-lived JWT token. Login to Rancher at https://localhost with credentials admin / Admin12345678!
